title: Is there light at the end of the tunnel for crude tankers in 2023?
section_path: Is there light at the end of the tunnel for crude tankers in 2023?
section_path_text: Is there light at the end of the tunnel for crude tankers in 2023?
summary:
As crude tanker rates reach new lows for 2023, we drill down into the drivers behind this, how this has shifted dynamics within the crude tanker market, as well as how support could materialise in the last quarter of the year. By Dylan Simpson
Crude tanker rates have been sliding across the board in the last couple of months. Since July, indices for the three main crude tanker classes have dropped by 20%, 15% and 30% for VLCCs, Suezmaxes and Aframaxes respectively, reaching new lows for 2023.
